reviews.review_description: Ok this game is fire but the pay wall is so annoying

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review. We ask for your continued interest and play.

reviews.review_description:
Overview
This game is a nice differentiation to its predecessor World War Toons. It features a 4v4 tank mayhem online with other players set in a futuristic setting. 

                                Lobby
The game starts in a cyber-themed room, where you can customize your character, buy currency and cosmetics, and customize you room and start the game. You are given a tag and a pre-determined name, which I’ll explain a bit later.
Most of the game’s replay value is shown through cosmetics, as there is a season pass with actually good rewards on the free tier and allows players to collect currency as they play the game and level up. The avatar cosmetics are also good, however there is a lack of variety of skins to choose which might offset some players. I like the amount of furniture and collectibles you can choose to put in your room and you can place anything free-form. You can also check out all the tanks in the room and customize the skins from the barrel, treads, and body. Being able to customize the walls and floors would make the room look more unique.

                        Multiplayer Lobby
The multiplayer lobby is where there are some issues with the game start to shine. I don’t care much about graphics, but I was able to clearly see a lot of pixels in the multiplayer lobby when I looked at signs and the targets for archery. In general the low-res was most noticeable in this area in the game and all the Quest headsets do have enough power to exceed the resolution scale. When trying  to friend people, you have to type in their tag which might be tedious to some players. It would help if you could directly friend request based off of their profile. The gesture to friend people only worked with one hand (the left one, I believe). Lastly, if you have a Meta  friend and you friend them on this game, but you also friend some strangers, the contrast between the Meta name and the random names they give you here can leave players very confused. Having all the names the same as the Meta Quest ones would help keep friendships in and outside of the game. The archery in the game also has no score counter/function to start the game, so it doesn’t have much of a use to be fun with players. The lobby doesn’t showcase the theme all too much and it is overall mostly grey. Having things like arcade carpet textures would make it lively and help it attach to the theme more.  Overall though, it serves its job when you want to find people.

                      Gameplay/Multiplayer
There isn’t any flaws with the actual gameplay aspects. My first game started before I finished the tutorial, but I got a feel for the controls. For text, I had to move a bit closer because of some of the resolution, but other that that I had no resolution issues here. It’s a 4v4 where everyone chooses a tank, with three different tank classes, Attack, Defense, and Support. Every tank has a crit point, which is the turret. While someone on your team uses one tank, you cannot use that tank, but you can change tanks every deploy. The balance on all tanks is very good, I have had no issue whatsoever with balancing and I have tried all the tanks. On the top of your tank there is a map which marks the capture point, the status, and the enemies and allies on the map. This map does take away the space from the top of your tank, so if there was a pull tab that made it adjustable, it could help some players. There are at least three maps and one game mode. The game mode requires people of one team to fully control the capture point at the middle which stays dormant. Games typically end relatively quickly overall, so a variety of game modes would help like a capture the flag game mode, as well as maybe an artifact one. Having coins around the map could easily encourage players to move around more. The backgrounds of the maps are pretty good, however some of the maps still don’t resemble the themes too much, and adding blue-ish shapes and patterns to the floors and the walls would make everything look more lively. While it doesn’t affect me, having an option to turn off voice lines is also a good option for the playerbase.

I believe the biggest issue in-game is that team Voice Chat is not enabled by default. This prevents players from sticking together and friending others as they can’t socialize unless they are in a party, and you would have to be in the lobby to friend people, because you cannot see people’s tags in-game. You also can’t tell if you would be playing with real players or with bots. I also had to rejoin one of my friends through the menu after every game since I was in a different lobby than my squad every time.
  
                                  Verdict
Overall, Ultimate League provides a definitive VR arcade tank expirience, as it is a very niece part of the VR industry and Quest platform. Its strongest areas are in its cosmetics and season pass grind, as well as the friendly characters and room customization. It takes the good aspects of World War Toons and combines it with a futuristic theme. If you are looking for something to just jump into, or a simple tank game, this serves it’s general purpose and I would recommend. I hope this was helpful.

                Extra Feedback/Suggestions
-Adding multiplayer and surface customization to rooms
-Turning on team voice chat by default
-Allow players to see Meta Quest names
-Help fix the friend gesture to work with both hands
-Increase the resolution of the text and icons
-Add scores to archery
-Add more gamemodes
-Daily rewards to keep players coming back
-Pull tab for in-game map
-Maybe have an option for manual reload like WWT
-Have an option to turn off announcer voice
-Have a leveling system for tanks to get exclusive cosmetics/currency/attachments that change function
-Add coins around the map (maybe for multiplayer lobby too)

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed review and feedback on Tank Arena: Ultimate League.
We are glad the recent patch resolved the issue with Meta and in-game nicknames displaying consistently. However, we understand your concerns about lobby resolution, friend adding, lack of default voice chat, and limited game modes. We will work on improving resolution, streamlining friend adding, enabling default voice chat, adding new modes, and enhancing room customization based on your valuable suggestions. Stay tuned for these improvements in future updates as we strive to provide the best experience based on player feedback.

Join our Discord channel and interact with us
https://discord.gg/taul

reviews.review_description: I love the way the controls feel and the tank designs and the way the cockpits look worth the money 10/10 underrated. The only complaint is the lack of customizing the cockpit

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for loving the game! We really appreciate it when players enjoy the game. 
I'll let the developers know that you've requested cockpit customization. We love all the suggestions we get and try to incorporate them into the game whenever we can. 
We also have a community on Discord, so swing on by if you get a chance. You can interact directly with developers there too!

Discord: https://discord.gg/taul

reviews.review_description: Please give me a refund my friend got one and we have had for the same amount of time please bro please give refund

developer_response.body:
We understand your disappointment with the current state of the game, and we apologize for the experience falling short of your expectations. We want you to know that we value your opinion and take your concerns seriously.

As of refunding the game, it isn't something that can be done on our side, but it must be processed through Meta Store. Sorry we can't be of more help on this part. 

Also, our team is constantly working on enhancing the game's content, features, and overall experience. While the game may have seemed underwhelming at first, we have exciting updates and additions planned for the near future.

If you have any additional suggestions or inquiries, we kindly invite you to visit our official Discord server https://discord.gg/taul, where our team can provide you with more prompt and detailed assistance.

We look forward to winning back your trust and providing you with an exceptional gaming experience in the near future.

reviews.review_title: Capture the Flag in a TANK!
reviews.review_description:
Sit in the driver's seat, with tanks featuring classes - support, assault, healers, etc. Virtual controls, or stick controls. 
Game plays its strengths to Capture the Flag, and can be a lot of fun joining in with other players to overpower the position on the map. Hermes being my favourite of the tanks to push the point and keep the team healed up. Community is supported by great staff, and they're looking into community feedback to help build the game further, some developments that could help improve include end of match stats and leaderboards. 
The lounge also is a great chill out space with darts, basketball hoops, and a soccer field with giant ball, which shows development that makes up this game is more than just the focus on main play. Come join the fun in the lounge, and arena!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed description of our game and the positive review! It always brings us joy to hear from our players and get feedbacks. 
We'll try to get the match stats and leaderboards incorporated into the game. 
As always, thank you for playing Tank Arena: Ultimate League and we hope to see you on the battlefield.

reviews.review_title: An Immersive VR Tank Battle Experience
reviews.review_description:
I recently had the pleasure of diving into this VR game, and it has quickly become one of my favorites. The game offers an exhilarating experience of tank battles with a unique twist - it's designed for four-player multiplayer, making it perfect for playing with friends.

The tank controls are incredibly intuitive, allowing for smooth navigation and strategic combat. The attention to detail in the tank mechanics and the battlefield environments is outstanding, providing a highly immersive experience. The combat is intense and requires teamwork, which adds a layer of strategy that keeps each session exciting and dynamic.

The four-player multiplayer mode is a standout feature. Coordinating with friends in real-time to outmaneuver opponents is both challenging and rewarding. The voice chat functionality is seamless, making communication easy and effective during heated battles. It's clear that the developers put a lot of effort into ensuring that the multiplayer aspect enhances the overall experience.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your insightful review of the VR tank battle game. It's great to hear that the immersive experience, intuitive controls, and engaging multiplayer aspect have made it one of your favorite games. Your feedback is valuable for both the developers and potential players.

reviews.review_description:
I was debating whether to give this game a 4 star or 5, but since there aren’t a lot of tank games in the meta quest store, I decided to give the game a chance. 

Reason: for starters, the tank battles were pretty intense and engage-able. Sure, I think it would need to improve in some areas. Like…I would get hit without feeling the effects of the hits and would die without knowing. That was a bit annoying, but I got the hang of it. 

Having to strategize with other players was also a plus for me. Usually most of these types of games are deathmatches, but knowing each other roles of the tanks helped working with teammates a lot more engaging. 

The aiming was a bit difficult and being used to the joysticks to manually playing needed time to adjust. But I think it was designed that way to make it feel more immersive. 

Social area was different…I wish there was a scoreboard for the arrow shooting or the darts. It got old really fast for me. But if they add the scoreboard, I think it’ll be much better. 

Me and a couple of my friends bought the game together, so it was worth playing together. 

Overall, I would recommend this game if you like strategic type of games and like to fool around with friends. I’m sure the game will improve with time. Hoping to see updates that improve the game.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your comprehensive review and constructive feedback. Your insights into the game's strengths and areas for improvement will undoubtedly aid the developers in enhancing the overall experience for all players.

reviews.review_description:
Overall it’s a really great game, it’s really fun to fight in tanks and be able to actually drive it instead of using the boring old joysticks, it’s a lot of fun having to get used to the tanks controls with a variety of different tanks that all have different abilities that the can activate



It’s a really but game but the capture the tower game mode is getting kinda boring, I’m hoping in the future that there will be more tanks and it would be really cool if there could be helicopters and planes to fly around the map and I’m hoping there’s more game modes

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts on the game. We're thrilled that you're enjoying the immersive tank driving experience and the variety of abilities across different tanks. 

 We ask for your continued interest and play.

Join our Discord channel and interact with us
https://discord.gg/taul

reviews.review_title: I Can’t wait for future updates!
reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love the lounge. Hanging out with friends, playing darts, and enjoying mini-games is a blast. I can’t wait for more events in the lounge. 
The talking cat, Mir, inside the tank is super cute, but more voice lines, animations, and customization options would make it even better.

It’d be awesome to see new game modes like a last-man-standing, a bigger battle royale, and a ranking system to compete with similar players. 

The tank graphics are top-notch, and the lounge is perfect for socializing. 
However, there are some server issues and the customization interface needs some tweaking. Tank combat could also be a bit more intense.

A tutorial mode for new players and better sound controls would be great additions. 

Despite a few minor issues, this game has huge potential with its amazing graphics and features. Can’t wait for future updates! Shoutout to the developers!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your enthusiastic review and for sharing your excitement about the game's potential. Your suggestions for future updates, such as additional lounge events, expanded customization options, new game modes, a tutorial system, and improved server stability, are valuable insights that can help shape the game's development roadmap.

reviews.review_description: This game is great, I find it very fun, but it doesn’t take a genius to realize through the bots that fill matches and the dead lounges that the player count is suffering. There’s plenty of room for updates and overall growth in the game, but it’s being held back by the price tag and probably the price tag alone. A game like this doesn’t need to cost as much as it does, because the model it’s based on is one usually focused primarily on income through cosmetics. You’ve got the grounds set up, several tanks and the avatar, plenty of wiggle room for customization, but the $20 price tag on a game that’s objectively not worth $20 up front is what’s crippling the opportunity for a larger player base. Brass tacks; if it was free, it’d definitely get a sizable influx in players, seeing as a fun, free game is hard to come by on VR these days.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the suggestion. I think we need to take a closer look at your suggestions for adding more users to the game. We ask for your suggestions and interest.

If you have any additional suggestions, we kindly invite you to visit our official Discord server https://discord.gg/taul

reviews.review_title: Fun - looking forward to future updates
reviews.review_description:
I've probably played about 20 games so far, and it's been pretty fun.  The base mechanics seem solid, and it's a fun vibe sitting in the tank and driving around.  It looks nice visually and has a consistent art style that works for the game.

The game could use some more game modes and ways to have fun in the tank that's not just the base 4v4 online game.  The online 4v4 is a fun experience, but the framework that was built has a lot of potential for single player experiences, cooperative modes, or more casual online versus modes.

There needs to be a post game stats screen with a way to send messages or friend requests.  I'd like to be able to see everyone's kills, deaths, assists, damage, healing done, damage taken, etc.  It would also be nice to be able to run custom games with 8 players.  I would assume the tryhards would like to find each other and play more competitively.  

I'd like to see the 4v4 game modes expanded with larger maps and capture points that rotate to different places.  It would make the game more strategic and force people to move around more.  Right now if one team is winning decisively the game kind of boils down to the defenders sitting on a point and shooting at the attackers through whatever choke point they have to get through.  The capture point moving would vary things up a bit and give some breathing room for the attackers to adjust.  

I think the game could also use a a power up system with on map pickups.  Things like damage boost, overshield, speed boost, fast reloads, etc.  Give people a reason to go places on the map that aren't the capture point.  Teams should also have a constant power up spawn at their base that spawns stronger items if your team is doing poorly.  Maybe base it on damage taken and give bonuses for deaths so it's predictable.  In close games it won't decide the outcome, but in lopsided games it should make it a bit more fun for everyone.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the detailed feedback and suggestions! We truly appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts on potential improvements and new modes that could enhance the overall gameplay experience. Your insights are invaluable, and we will carefully consider them as we plan future updates and expansions to the game.

reviews.review_title: Tank is fun, but Lounge is so much fun
reviews.review_description:
The main game where you ride tanks was incredibly enjoyable, as you could pilot various tanks and use spectacular skills. In the lounge, I could meet and chat with other people, play pranks with toys, and even play a hand soccer game called Mega Ball. The lounge is insanely fun. I plan to have fun with people in the lounge every day.
I want to make friends in the lounge.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review. It's really fun to spend time with friends in the lounge. We ask for your continued interest and play.

reviews.review_description:
Nice game
Fun and fundamentally challenging tank game.
I think the lounge is so funny. lol

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review. We hope you have a great time in the lounge. We ask for your continued interest and play.

reviews.review_description: Fun game exited to see what the future updates have in store.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to leave a review and share your thoughts with us. We’re grateful for your support and appreciate your feedback on our game.

We're truly sorry to hear that your experience didn't meet your expectations and that you felt let down. We understand your concerns about the limited game modes and the pay-to-play elements, and we're actively working to address these issues.

Please rest assured that we have many updates planned to introduce more content and enhance the gameplay experience. We're committed to adding new game modes, features, and improvements to ensure the game becomes more enjoyable for everyone. Your feedback is invaluable in guiding these updates.

We also apologize for any inconvenience caused by the current limitations, such as the mission requiring purchased items to progress. Our goal is to create a fair and engaging environment for all players, and we're working on adjustments to achieve this balance.

We’re dedicated to making the game better with every update and hope to meet your expectations soon.

reviews.review_description: Me and my friends bought the game together and playing together was really fun. I think I'll play often when I have time.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review. We ask for your continued interest and play.
